content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

December 21, 2018


Claudia Quintana
Vallejo City Attorney
555 Santa Clara Street
Vallejo, CA 94590

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. A-18-245

Dear Ms. Quintana:

        This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Vallejo City Councilmember
Jesus Malgapo regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act.

        Also, please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) I
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for
additional advice.

        Additionally, the Commission does not provide advice regarding past conduct. (Regulation
18329(b)(8)(A).) Accordingly, nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any past
conduct, and any conclusions contained in this letter apply only to prospective actions.

QUESTION

        Does the Act prohibit City Councilmember Malgapo from taking part in a series of
upcoming economic development and land use decisions related to the Blue Rock Springs Golf
Course (the "Golf Course"), which is located within 500 feet of his residence?

CONCLUSION

        Yes. We do not find sufficient facts to indicate that there will be no reasonably foreseeable
measurable impact on the official's property. Councilmember Malgapo is therefore prohibited from
making, participating in making, or using his position to influence any decisions regarding the Golf
Course.


________________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-18-245
Page No. 2

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

The Golf Course is owned by the City, and is bisected by Columbus Parkway, which divides
the property into East and West Sides. Councilmember Malgapo's residence is located within 500
feet of the tip of the West side of the Golf Course.

The City Council has discussed how to eliminate the current city subsidy of operational debt
incurred on behalf of the Golf Course while maintaining the property as a public course. The City
prepared a request for proposal, seeking the development of one or both sides of the property and
ways in which the Golf Course might generate revenue to pay off the debt.

The City Council selected Greg Norman Golf. The decision was based, among other things,
on its vision to combine residential development with the Golf Course's preservation as a public
course. A new course is proposed, though it is unknown where, within the existing blueprint, the
new course will be located. The City entered into an Exclusive Right to Negotiate with Greg
Norman Golf.

Among other things, the City Council anticipates consideration of the following items
relating to the Golf Course at future meetings:

• A purchase-sale agreement between the City, the golf course designer, and a residential
development partner for approximately 70-to-100 acres within the existing 225-acre
footprint of the Golf Course. It is currently not known exactly where those 70-to-100
acres will be located within and in relation to the rest of the existing course. Any
agreement of this type would go before the City Council for approval.

• A development agreement between the City and the residential development partner for
the residential development of the 70-to-100 acres. A master plan will go to the Planning
Commission first, and then to the City Council for review.

• A design-build contract with the golf course designer for the renovation and
development of a new 18-hole course and construction of a new east preliminary
clubhouse on the remaining non-residential land.

• Syar Industries operates a mining quarry located on the opposite side of the East Course.
A revenue-share agreement with Syar Industries, as the adjoining landowner, which
includes a deed restriction on the remaining Golf Course property to ensure it remains
used for golf.

• Long-term license and operation agreements for the new golf operation and clubhouse.

Greg Norman Golf representatives stated that existing green buffer spaces, which adjoin
existing residential developments, are likely to remain. As a result, the adjoining golf course space
closest to existing residential developments would remain a green "buffer" zone. No applications,
location details, or designs have been determined or submitted, nor have any maps been presented
to the City Council.

File No. A-18-245
Page No. 3

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using
his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial
interest. A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental decision if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on one or more of the public
official's interests set forth in Section 87103. Specific to the provided facts, the following interests
are relevant:

• An interest in real property in which the officer has a direct or indirect interest of $2,000
or more. (Section 87103(b).)

• An interest in the official's personal finances, including those of the official's immediate
family. (Section 87103.) An official always has an interest in his or her personal
finances.²

Based on the provided facts, Councilmember Malgapo has a real property interest in his
house, assuming he has an interest of $2,000 or more.

Foreseeability and Materiality

Foreseeability standards vary depending on whether an interest is explicitly involved in a
governmental decision. An official's financial interest is explicitly involved in a decision, and is
presumed to be reasonably foreseeable, if the interest is a named party in, or the subject of, a
decision before the official or the official's agency.³ (Regulation 18701(a).) For a financial interest
that is not explicitly involved in the decision at issue, the financial effect of the decision on an
official's interest is reasonably foreseeable if it can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more
than hypothetical or theoretical. (Regulation 18701(b).) Councilmember Malgapo's interests are not
explicitly involved in the decisions at issue.

Regulation 18702.2 contains the materiality standards for the foreseeable effect of a decision
on an official's real property interest. Specific to the provided facts, Regulation 18702.2(a)(11)
provides that a financial effect is material where an official considers any decision affecting real
property value located within 500 feet of the property line of the official's residential real property.
Notwithstanding this prohibition, the Commission may provide written advice allowing an official
to participate under these circumstances if the Commission determines that there are sufficient facts
to indicate that there will be no reasonably foreseeable measurable impact on the official's property.

_______
² While an official also has an interest in his or her personal finances, including those of an immediate family
member (Section 87103), an effect on an official's personal finances is not considered separately where the decision
affects the official's interest in a business entity or real property. (Regulation 18702.5(c).) Therefore, we do not analyze
Councilmember Malgapo's interest in his personal finances.

³ Regulation 18701(a) establishes that a financial interest is the subject of a proceeding if the decision involves
the issuance, renewal, approval, denial, or revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the
financial interest, and includes any governmental decision affecting a real property financial interest as described in
Regulation 18702.2(a)(1)-(6).

File No. A-18-245
Page No. 4

Based on the provided facts, Councilmember Malgapo's home is located within 500 feet of the Golf Course. You indicated that no applications, location plans, or designs have been determined or submitted and no maps have been presented to the City Council. Further, you stated that the location is currently unknown for the 70-to-100 acres designated for residential development. Therefore, given the magnitude of the decisions and without additional information regarding the specifics of the project, we do not find sufficient facts to indicate that there will be no reasonably foreseeable measurable impact on the Councilmember Malgapo's property.

Accordingly, Councilmember Malgapo is prohibited from making, participating in making, or using his position to influence any decisions regarding the development of the Golf Course.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Sara K. Puricelli
Counsel, Legal Division

SKP:jgl
